mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate level is inversely related to S&P 500 forward returns: low unemployment signals tight labor markets, wage inflation pressures, and eventual monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations. When UNRATE is below its trailing 12-month average, forward 20-day returns tend to be negative.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce positive hit rate in lowest UNRATE quartile.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, the cost of capital increases sufficiently to reduce corporate investment and consumer spending, leading to lower earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return after triggers is not negative or not significantly different from baseline; more than 50% of triggered instances yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates an amplified negative signal for equities: weakening real estate activity combined with tightening monetary policy indicates a sharp slowdown in residential investment and consumer wealth, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
regime_hint: late economic cycle with tightening central bank
failure_condition: Average forward return in interaction-triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the joint signal does not outperform either single-series signal in terms of Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2%), rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand without immediate overheating risk, thus corporate earnings improve and equity prices rise over the next 20 trading days. The signal flips sign when inflation exceeds 2%.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY < 2% and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: In regimes with CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, RSAFS growth does not produce statistically significant positive forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03, p > 0.10) over any 2-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals the onset of a production contraction that quickly reduces corporate earnings and raises recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after trigger is not negative; less than 55% of triggered events produce negative returns; the signal fails to predict NBER recession start dates.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining real GDP (GDPC1) and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) violates Okun's Law and signals a broad economic contraction, producing persistent negative pressure on equities over 60 trading days as earnings fall and risk aversion rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0 and UNRATE month-over-month rise > 0.2 pp
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative in periods when both triggers are active; the interaction term in a regression (GDPC1*UNRATE) is not significant at 5% level over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by rising FEDFUNDS and CPI (CPIAUCSL) above target, further interest rate increases compress equity valuations by discounting future cash flows at higher rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The effect intensifies when both series rise together.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month moving average rising and CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increasing, CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%)
failure_condition: The joint trigger does not produce negative average forward returns; the signal fails to differentiate between tightening and non-tightening regimes (i.e., returns are not significantly more negative in tightening periods).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) month-over-month growth immediately signals robust consumption momentum, lifting corporate revenue expectations and driving short-term S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days as market participants adjust earnings forecasts upward.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth is not positive; the signal produces negative average returns in more than 40% of months with above-median RSAFS growth.
